danger_signals.visual_form: Incoming energy bolts and attacks from opponent ST units
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring defensive interception or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct ST designs (e.g., ST Cobra) with visible weapon systems
danger_signals.meaning: Presence of hostile Standing Tank combatant

danger_signals.visual_form: Specific targeting zones on enemy ST units
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability exposure; indicates enemy readiness to deal damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Return of defeated tyrant as boss encounter
danger_signals.meaning: World-state threat: return of tyranny and destabilization

danger_signals.visual_form: Alien race faction presence in Solar System
danger_signals.meaning: Existential threat to Earth; foreign conquest attempt

safety_signals.visual_form: Player-controlled Standing Tank with functional weapon systems
safety_signals.meaning: Player has defensive capability and offensive readiness

safety_signals.visual_form: Power meters filling for treble shot (three-charge system)
safety_signals.meaning: Preparation phase; player can build toward enhanced attack capacity

safety_signals.visual_form: Player shooting down incoming enemy fire
Tutorial scenario with instructor Rola guidance
safety_signals.meaning: Safe learning environment; no existential threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: ST Falcon / ST Tornado standing tanks
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: ST Falcon commanded by Mike Anderson (protagonist unit); ST Tornado piloted by Carol Eugene (unlocked unit)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Earth setting; Solar System defense staging

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Anubis as returning boss enemy; associated ST units piloted by 'chiefs' in Battle Game
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Post-defeat return scenario; re-establishment of tyrannical rule attempt

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Eltorian-piloted ST units with distinct attack patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Extra-solar origin; active invasion of Solar System

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual ST unit pilots with unique designs and strategies
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each chief ST has distinct appearance, weak points, and combat behavior
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hierarchical challenge structure in single-player campaign

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-Battle Clash: Age of Prosperity (3 years prior)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Implied stable Earth condition; absence of tyranny
Emperor Anubis present as active boss encounter; resumption of threat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Renewed tyrannical oppression; demands for one-on-one duels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alien race presence in Solar System; multiple Eltorian ST units
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Organized conquest attempt; one-on-one combat engagements against Earth defenders

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Continuous ST-vs-ST combat scenarios across Battle, Time Trial, and Combat modes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mike Anderson and allies engage in rapid-succession duels; unlocking ST Tornado indicates victory progression

environmental_storytelling.example: ST Falcon (player default): balanced defense/offense; ST Cobra (enemy variant): distinct visual signature; ST Tornado (unlocked): specialized loadout with ten-beam storage but reduced defense—each conveys faction allegiance and strategic role
Progression from Battle Game chiefs → Emperor Anubis → Eltorian alien race creates escalating threat narrative; visual presentation of each opponent signals world-state escalation
Each enemy ST has visible targeting zones; their exposure communicates enemy vulnerability windows and reinforces mechanical narrative of finding exploitable weaknesses
ST Tornado reward for single-player completion signals victory state transition and player progression through world narrative
Player gunner position within ST Falcon cockpit establishes player role as defensive crew member within a larger military apparatus

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fire projectiles requiring interception; exhibit distinct attack patterns and strategies; expose weak points for targeting
Accumulate energy bolts up to three charges; fire treble shot when power meter filled
Intercept incoming projectiles; deflect special attacks with energy bolts
Store up to ten energy beams; fire individually; exhibits reduced defensive capability versus ST Falcon
Engages in one-on-one duel; represents narrative climax of return threat
Combat engagement as conquering alien force; distinct ST pilots with invasion-aligned strategies

notes: Metal Combat employs minimal explicit visual faction markers due to light-gun gameplay constraints and SNES hardware limitations. World-state communication relies heavily on narrative context (Anubis return, alien invasion) rather than environmental visual transformation. Faction identity is primarily conveyed through ST unit design differentiation and boss/enemy encounter sequencing. The game's mechanical systems (weak points, energy charging, projectile interception) serve as implicit world-state indicators—player capability to counter enemy attacks reflects engagement with destabilized world conditions. No explicit color-coded faction systems are documented. Training mode with Rola instructor represents safe-state narrative contrast to main campaign threats.
